narrative:
Seasonably cool conditions persist today with a considerable amount of cloud cover. Still, it will be a pleasant day for mid-June. Wednesday is partly sunny and warmer. On Thursday, scattered afternoon showers and thunderstorms can be expected as temperatures move another notch higher. Unsettled weather will continue on Friday before we get a good-looking Saturday.

Pleasant Through Midweek

Last Updated Tue June 16 at 4:02PM by Jack Kendrick

Seasonably cool conditions persist today with a considerable amount of cloud cover. Still, it will be a pleasant day for mid-June. Wednesday is partly sunny and warmer. On Thursday, scattered afternoon showers and thunderstorms can be expected as temperatures move another notch higher. Unsettled weather will continue on Friday before we get a good-looking Saturday.

Forecast Discussion

A stationary front situated just to our south today will return northward as a warm front tonight. This will keep some clouds around but should not detract much from a nice Tuesday.

Wednesday will be warmer with more sunshine.

A cold front will approach from the northwest on Thursday. This will generate scattered showers and thunderstorms during the afternoon and evening.

Complicating matters will be a tropical disturbance that could develop off the Texas Gulf coast and then get picked up and track through the Deep South/Southeast during Thursday night and Friday.

The lion’s share of the rain with this system will probably stay just south of the High Country and Southwest Virginia, but it could be a close call. Regardless, with the front stuck in the area and waiting for the tropical low to go by, we will be required to maintain showers through Friday before clearing commences for an outstanding start to the weekend.

For “Summer Solstice Sunday”, an isolated pop-up shower or thundershower will be back in the cards.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Latest Insights
Monday Message for June 15, 2026

It's the pre-summer solstice edition of the Monday Message, available for subscribers. El Niño has officially arrived, and will have significant implications for our weather heading into fall and winter.

Jun 15, 2026
El Niño Has Officially Arrived

El Niño has officially arrived, as described in the latest Climate Prediction Center ENSO discussion.

This will have significant implications on fall and winter temperature and precipitation in the Southeastern United States. While our region continues to experience significant drought conditions, above-normal precipitation will ultimately make a comeback during fall and winter.

Jun 14, 2026
Monday Message for June 8, 2026

This week's Monday Message highlights a week of hit-or-miss showers and thundershowers that will be returning to the region in typical June fashion. Also, we discuss the movie "Pressure", in theaters now.
